etymology.description: 武(武) (wǔ, "martial") denotes martial arts or military affairs. 林(林) (lín, "forest") figuratively represents a vast gathering or community. Together, 武林(武林) (wǔlín) conveys the notion of a community or world centered around martial arts.
etymology.interpretation: 武(武) (wǔ, "martial") provides the context—martial arts. 林(林) (lín, "forest") suggests a vast, dense collection. The 'forest' metaphorically alludes to a flourishing community, much as trees form a part of a dense forest. Therefore, 武林(武林) (wǔlín) depicts the world or society where those skilled in martial arts gather, akin to a cultural community or network involving martial arts practitioners and enthusiasts.

parts.english: forest; gathering; subset when referring to 'circle of people'

examples.chinese: 在武林中(在武林中)，许多高手都被尊敬(許多高手都被尊敬)。
examples.pinyin: Zài wǔlín zhōng, xǔduō gāoshǒu dōu bèi zūnjìng.
examples.english: In the martial arts circles, many masters are respected.

examples.chinese: 他梦想成为武林中的一员(他夢想成為武林中的一員)。
examples.pinyin: Tā mèngxiǎng chéngwéi wǔlín zhōng de yīyuán.
examples.english: He dreams of becoming a member of the martial arts circles.

examples.chinese: 江湖险恶(江湖險惡)，武林高手随时要小心(武林高手隨時要小心)。
examples.pinyin: Jiānghú xiǎn'è, wǔlín gāoshǒu suíshí yào xiǎoxīn.
examples.english: The martial arts world is dangerous, and martial arts masters must always be cautious.
